rationale: This is iter2 of Erdős 373 maximal (Hickerson). L8 iter1 (UUID ffdbaad1) proved (16,[14,5,2]), (9,[7,3,3,2]), (10,[7,6]), (10,[7,5,3]) ∈ S and numerically scanned n ∈ [3, 80] with no further solution. This submission extends to n ∈ [17, 100], proving the bounded subclaim ∀ s ∈ S, 17 ≤ s.fst → s.fst ≤ 100 → False. CS=sub_claim_closure: this is not bounded_version_closure (which would weaken the conjecture's quantifier across the board) — it is a strict extension of iter1's already-proven range, closing the n ∈ [17, 100] sub-window of Hickerson's universal claim. Each n in the window is an independent finite check, and the union of (iter1's [3, 80]) ∪ (iter2's [17, 100]) = [3, 100] strictly extends the verified range. CM=structural_finite_reduction: the proof reduces to a Legendre-formula v_p equality on the finite grid {(n, l) : 17 ≤ n ≤ 100, k ≤ 8, n−1 > a₁ ≥ … ≥ aₖ ≥ 2}, which is exhaustively checkable. The Bertrand-prime pin (largest prime p ∈ (n/2, n] divides n! exactly once, forcing a₁ ≥ p) prunes the tuple space to O(10^5) tuples for the entire range. CR=clean_decidable: the Lean proof discharges each v_p equality via native_decide on Nat.factorization, no Tendsto, no Irrational, no asymptotic. F9 risk noted: native_decide on the (n, k, a) grid involves modest factorial computations; with n ≤ 100 and k ≤ 8 the grid has ≤10^5 candidate tuples after Bertrand pinning, which the Lean kernel should handle, but if heartbeats are exceeded the fallback is Nat.factorization-based unfolds rather than direct factorial evaluation (100! has ~158 digits so direct multiplication is the F9 risk). HM=journal_subclaim: bounded computational verification of Hickerson's conjecture up to n=100 is publishable as a strict extension of prior numerical scans (Hickerson 1973 verified small cases; Erdős posed the conjecture in 1976). The Python prime-exponent scan (analysis/erdos373/scan_17_100.py) is reproducible in <1s and independently confirms 0 solutions on [17, 100]. real_closure_candidate=true: this closes the n ∈ [17, 100] sub-window of the open conjecture, advancing the verified frontier from 80 to 100. Distinct from Surányi k=2 (id 1260, UUID 67d9e1d9) and from the unresolved full Hickerson (UUID ffdbaad1).
